sections.paragraph:
Tav (Tau), the twenty-third [reckoning שׂ and שׁ for two letters] and last letter of the alphabet, when used as a numeral denoting four hundred. As to the signification of the name see under the word תָּו .

As to pronunciation, ת without Dagesh is an aspirated letter, and seems to have a lisping sound, like Gr. θ, and th English. When it has Dagesh lene (תּ) it is a slender t; as to its difference from ט, see page 316, A . To this there answers in Arabic ت, rarely ث, as in תָּקַף, ثقف. It is sometimes interchanged with Shin (p. 797, A ) and Tet (p. 316, B ), and it has even some relation to the breathings (א, ה), see Hebr. Gramm. page 101, note; also, אֹוב, שׁוּב, תּוּב to return; אָוָה and תָּוָה to dwell; also, to mark out, and so often in Arabic.
